query: EU AI Act AI Omnibus annex III high-risk systems compliance deadline extension annex I regulated products deadline official text 2026
a_issues: Mostly secondary sources rather than official text. One result snippet (compliancehub.wiki) appears stale or incorrect by listing the original dates for Annex III (2 Aug 2026) and Annex I (2 Aug 2027), which could confuse the model. The Commission page snippet says the proposal was 'adopted on 19 November 2025,' which is imprecise because that is the Commission proposal date, not final legal adoption; however it also correctly mentions the political agreement and the extended transition period to 2 Aug 2028.
b_issues: No official EU source or official-text result is surfaced despite the query explicitly asking for official text. The top result is a CSA research note, a non-official secondary source. One result says agreement was reached on 6 May and confirmed on 13 May, while another says 7 May, creating date inconsistency about the political agreement. The result about the Machinery Regulation adds detail that may be accurate but is peripheral to the asked deadline issue and could distract. Overall source reliability is weaker than A because it lacks an EC/EU page or EUR-Lex/Council document.
reasoning: The question asks about the AI Omnibus extension of compliance deadlines for Annex III high-risk systems and Annex I regulated products, ideally from official text. Both A and B contain the key deadline facts: Annex III moved to 2 December 2027 and Annex I regulated-product systems to 2 August 2028. However, A better enables a correct answer because it includes an official European Commission page ('AI Act | Shaping Europe’s digital future') explicitly stating that high-risk AI systems embedded into regulated products have an extended transition period until 2 August 2028 due to the AI omnibus, alongside reputable legal commentary repeating the Annex III and Annex I dates. B has the deadline facts too, but all surfaced sources are secondary/non-official and it contains minor inconsistency on the agreement date (6 May vs 7 May). Since the task is to answer correctly from the returned content alone, A provides stronger and more reliable grounding, even though it also includes one misleading low-quality snippet.
